video_title: Managing Stress: A Group Discussion with Dr. Kensa Gunter | Youth Mental Wellness Series
video_description:
Stress is real. And it's okay to talk about it. 

In this episode of the Hawks At Home Youth Mental Wellness Series, Coach Step, Coach Narcis, and Dr. Kensa Gunter sit down for an open and honest conversation about managing stress. Together they share practical strategies, encourage healthy habits, and remind young people that talking about mental wellness isn't a sign of weakness — it's a sign of strength.

You're not alone in what you're feeling. 

Whether you're dealing with pressure at school, on the court, or at home — this conversation was made for you.

👇 Drop a comment below: What's one thing that helps you manage stress when life gets heavy?

video_title: What is Journaling? with Dr. Kensa Gunter | Hawks At Home Youth Mental Wellness Series
video_description:
Your thoughts deserve a place to land. 

In this episode of the Hawks At Home Youth Mental Wellness Series, Dr. Kensa Gunter — NBA Director of Mental Health & Wellness — introduces journaling as a powerful tool for self-reflection and emotional expression. Dr. Gunter breaks it down in a way that's accessible and real for young people, sharing simple ways to process thoughts, understand feelings, and build healthy habits through writing.

You don't have to have the right words. You just have to start. 

Whether you're working through something big or just learning to check in with yourself — this one meets you right where you are.

video_title: Robert Parish Punches Bill Laimbeer in Game 5 of 1987 Eastern Conference Finals
video_description:
Robert Parish let his fists fly on Bill Laimbeer during Game 5 of the 1987 Eastern Conference Finals between the Boston Celtics and the “Bad Boy” Detroit Pistons. As Parish explains, the moment was an emotional tipping point after he and Laimbeer exchanged words and physical play throughout the series. Parish reveals exactly what was going through his mind and body as he threw the punches – and somehow avoided a technical foul, let alone an ejection 👀

video_title: Retired NBA player Leo Rautins’ First NBA Preseason Game Turned Into a BRAWL | This one time…
video_description:
This one time… Leo Rautins found out very quickly just how tough the NBA could be.

In Episode 02, Leo looks back on the physicality of the league in the 1980s, how the game compares to today, and the wild story of his first preseason game as a member of the Philadelphia 76ers against Boston — a game that went from hard-nosed basketball to a full-on brawl.

From old-school intensity to Beantown chaos, Leo breaks down what it was really like to step into the NBA when every possession came with a little extra smoke.

Watch the full story now.
